speaker: [player]
text: Actually... I want to hear the Grand Hymn.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Oh no.

speaker: Vermia
text: Oh YES.

speaker: Dirtus
text: I CALL PERCUSSION DUTY.

speaker: Grubwin
text: There is no percussion in the Grand Hymn.

speaker: Dirtus
text: I will create percussion. With my hands. On this desk.

speaker: Mawla
text: I'll sing it if Vermia starts. But I'm not doing the harmony part alone again. Last time a teacher walked by.

speaker: Vermia
text: She looked MOVED.

speaker: Mawla
text: She looked confused. There's a difference.
Vermia clears her throat. She stands straighter. Her expression shifts into something genuinely serious — reverent, almost. You realize with mild alarm that she is actually about to sing.

speaker: Vermia
text: O Scatha, beneath the soil and stone / You coil and crown, you dwell alone / But we who scratch the surface thin / We call you up, we call you in—

speaker: Dirtus
text: *desk percussion intensifies*

speaker: Grubwin
text: Stop.

speaker: Dirtus
text: I cannot stop. I am inspired.
Mawla joins on the second verse with a surprisingly good alto harmony. Grubwin, sighing deeply, mouths along to words he clearly knows by heart. You watch all of this happen.
It's ridiculous. It's also — somehow — kind of beautiful? The melody is actually genuinely nice. Vermia wrote it herself.

speaker: Vermia
text: —and RISE, Scatha, RISE from the deep / From loam and legend, from earthen sleep / We offer words, we offer rhyme / We call to you across all time—!
Silence. Then Dirtus hits the desk one final time.

speaker: Dirtus
text: That's it. That's the one. That's the energy we need for the summoning.

speaker: Grubwin
text: We are not doing a summoning.

speaker: Vermia
text: Did you feel it, [player]? Did you feel the resonance?
You think about it honestly. The melody did something. You're not sure what.
